title: 'Totally obliterated': Trump announces US strike on military targets at Iran’s key oil export island
content: President Trump announced the US conducted a powerful bombing raid on Iran's Kharg Island, destroying military targets while sparing oil infrastructure. This action follows Iran's interference with shipping in the Strait of Hormuz and escalates the conflict involving the US, Israel, and Iran.

title: Iran allows two India‑flagged LPG carriers to transit Strait of Hormuz amid conflict
content: Iran has granted safe passage to two India-flagged LPG carriers through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical energy route. This assurance comes from Iran's Ambassador to India, who emphasized the strong friendship and shared interests between the two nations. The move occurs amidst regional tensions and follows Iran's criticism of US policy on Russian oil imports.

title: 'Forced to join vessel before war': Mumbai engineer killed in tanker attack near Iraq
content: Tragedy struck as Indian seafarer Deonandan Prasad Singh lost his life during an assault on the oil tanker MT Safesea Vishnu near the treacherous Khor Al Zubair Port in Iraq. Reports suggest that Singh's family claims he was compelled to return to the ship merely days prior to the fatal incident, despite being assigned a shore-based position.

title: ‘What’s their connection with Guwahati?’: Ex-India cricketer questions RR strategy
content: Aakash Chopra has questioned the Rajasthan Royals' decision to host three IPL matches in Guwahati, citing the significant geographical distance and lack of historical connection. Despite Riyan Parag's Assamese roots, Chopra finds the move unusual for a team representing Rajasthan, as the franchise shifts a substantial portion of its home fixtures away from Jaipur.

title: Accenture CEO Julie Sweet to company’s employees: If you want a promotion, you need to ...
content: Accenture CEO Julie Sweet has mandated that employees must demonstrate proficiency in using AI tools to be considered for promotions. This requirement reflects the consultancy's integration of AI into its core operations, a shift Sweet likens to the adoption of computers in the past. The company has invested significantly in reskilling its workforce to adapt to these evolving workplace technologies.

title: 'No one can tell Dhoni to sit out': Ex-CSK star's bold claim on dressing room reality
content: Former India batter Subramaniam Badrinath believes Chennai Super Kings management faces an immense challenge in dropping MS Dhoni from the playing XI. Despite Dhoni's role yet to be finalized for IPL 2026, Badrinath asserts that even coach Stephen Fleming would find it impossible to tell the legendary captain he's not playing or is just an impact player, given his stature.

title: Palantir CEO Alex Karp admits using Anthropic's Claude after Pentagon ban
content: Palantir CEO Alex Karp confirmed continued use of Anthropic's Claude AI models despite the Pentagon labeling the company a supply chain risk.  The Department of Defense is planning a phase-out, but acknowledges a transition will take time, with potential exemptions for mission-critical operations. This highlights the challenges in disentangling deeply embedded AI systems from defense operations.

title: US offers up to $10m for info on Mojtaba Khamenei and other key Iran leaders
content: The US State Department has announced a $10 million reward for information on Iran's new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, and other senior officials. This follows recent retaliatory strikes and escalating regional tensions. President Trump, meanwhile, claimed Iran is nearing surrender and vowed to militarily dismantle its leadership, calling them "deranged scumbags" during ongoing intense exchanges of drone and missile attacks.

title: Palantir CEO Alex Karp reacts to Iran's attack on three Amazon data centers
content: Palantir CEO Alex Karp asserts AI grants the US and allies a decisive edge against Iran, highlighting the "uniquely American" AI revolution. He views Iran's attacks on data centers as a strategic move in modern warfare. Karp also alluded to Palantir's Project Maven's potential role in intelligence operations, though he declined to confirm specific involvement.

title: Why the US is sending assault ship USS Tripoli, thousands of marines to the Middle East
content: The US is significantly expanding its military presence in the Middle East, deploying thousands of Marines and the amphibious assault ship USS Tripoli amid escalating tensions with Iran. This move aims to deter further Iranian aggression, protect vital shipping lanes like the Strait of Hormuz and provide the US with greater response options in the rapidly widening regional conflict.

title: 'Ustaad Bhagat Singh' first review out: Pawan Kalyan’s entry scene called ‘Paisa Vasool’
content: Pawan Kalyan's 'Ustaad Bhagat Singh' is creating massive buzz ahead of its March 19th release. Early reviews hail it as 'Paisa Vasool,' with advance bookings already crossing Rs 120 crore. Andhra Pradesh has approved a temporary ticket price hike, and special early morning benefit shows are planned, promising a grand opening for the action entertainer.

title: NFL Trade Rumors: Nick Bosa’s San Francisco 49ers linked to $156M Buffalo Bills defensive end
content: The San Francisco 49ers are strongly linked to signing veteran defensive end Joey Bosa. He may play alongside his younger brother, Nick Bosa, who is also a defensive end. While Joey’s experience and talent are undeniable, his age and the 49ers’ 2026 strategy of preferring younger talent could become potential obstacles to a right priced deal. Else, the fans and analysts have always wanted to brothers play for one team.

title: Taylor Swift’s $2B news signals power future with Travis Kelce
content: Travis Kelce has confirmed he will return to the Kansas City Chiefs for another NFL season, signing a one year deal reportedly worth up to $15 million. The veteran tight end says his decision was driven by his continued love for football. Kelce also credited Taylor Swift’s passion for her craft as inspiration. With the couple expected to marry before training camp, both stars remain committed to thriving in their respective careers rather than slowing down.

title: Fact check: Is Travis Hunter paying $150K monthly to wife Leanna Lenee?
content: Travis Hunter, the Jacksonville Jaguars star player, and his wife, Leanna Lenee, have been married for less than a year and have already found themselves involved in a scandalous debate after rumors of the star player being cheated on by his newly wed wife sparked chaos. Amid this, a clip of Travis Hunter allegedly saying he has to pay her a massive amount monthly to her has sparked chaos.

title: Tanushree Dutta makes shocking claim about exploitation in showbiz: 'Many newcomers get abused...'
content: Tanushree Dutta has once again spoken about the darker side of the entertainment industry, warning aspiring actors about manipulation, exploitation and the dangers of blindly trusting people who promise opportunities. The actress said one of the reasons she speaks openly about such issues is to warn young aspirants who come to the industry chasing dreams.

title: Abrar controversy: List of Pak players playing for Indian-owned teams
content: Pakistan spinner Abrar Ahmed’s signing by Sunrisers Leeds in The Hundred created a flutter, though the BCCI said overseas leagues are outside its control. Bought for £190,000 by the Sun Group-owned franchise, Abrar’s deal drew attention as Pakistani players remain absent from the IPL since 2009. Despite that, several Pakistan cricketers have played for IPL-linked teams in global T20 leagues.

title: 'They are not fishing': Thousands of Chinese boats form unusual patterns near Taiwan
content: Thousands of Chinese fishing boats formed unusual geometric patterns in the East China Sea, raising questions about potential military or strategic mobilization. Experts suggest these large-scale, coordinated gatherings, unlike normal fishing, may indicate preparations for crisis scenarios involving Taiwan or Japan.

title: War upends life as LPG crisis hits not only eateries & hostels but also temples
content: The ongoing conflict between Iran, the US, and Israel has triggered a severe LPG cylinder shortage across India, leading to black marketing, price hikes, and widespread business closures. Restaurants and educational institutions are forced to reduce operations and meals, while families resort to traditional cooking methods to cope with the crisis.

title: Refining relations: India pumps up goodwill as neighbours seek emergency fuel
content: India is responding to urgent fuel requests from Nepal, Bangladesh, and Sri Lanka. These nations face shortages due to Middle East supply disruptions. India, a major refiner, is assessing its domestic needs before deciding on exports. This situation highlights India's growing role as a regional energy partner.

title: Maha anti-conversion bill proposes 7-year jail, Rs 1 lakh fine
content: Maharashtra has introduced a stringent bill to curb religious conversions through coercion, fraud, or marriage inducement. The proposed law mandates a 60-day notice for conversions and imposes severe penalties, including a seven-year jail term for unlawful conversions linked to marriage. Critics argue the bill infringes on personal freedoms and could foster fear, particularly concerning inter-faith unions.

title: Ready to work legally if ban on Maoist party lifted: Devji
content: Top Maoist Devuji, who recently joined the mainstream, urged Telangana CM Revanth Reddy to advocate for lifting the ban on CPI (Maoist). He stated the party would operate legally and disband the PLGA if granted political space. Devuji also called for the release of jailed Maoist leaders and support for their families.

title: Crude tankers from Russia, Liberia on way to Indian ports
content: Two crude oil tankers have successfully navigated the Strait of Hormuz, heading towards Indian ports. This offers some relief to energy supplies. Several tankers carrying Russian oil are also rerouting to India. Shipping operations in the Gulf remain challenging, with increased costs due to diversions and higher insurance premiums.

title: Standard Chartered responds to viral rumours in UAE emirate amid Iran war
content: Amidst escalating Iran vs US-Israel war, Standard Chartered clarified reports of a Dubai office evacuation, stating it extended existing work-from-home measures as a precaution. The bank emphasised its regional operations remain active. This comes as other financial institutions also implement safety protocols, highlighting Dubai's crucial role as a financial hub facing geopolitical risks.

title: ‘Putin might be helping them’: Trump hints Russia could be aiding Iran
content: President Trump suggested Vladimir Putin might be aiding Iran in its conflict, drawing a parallel to US support for Ukraine. This comes after Trump previously questioned a reporter on similar reports.  Meanwhile, a US envoy stated Russia denied sharing intelligence with Iran, though expressed hope this remains true.

title: Crude shock! Investors lose Rs 34L cr since start of US-Iran war; where is your money safe?
content: The numbers are staggering: on February 27 the combined market capitalisation of BSE-listed companies was Rs 46,325,200.41 crore. As of March 13, 2025, it has fallen to Rs 42,939,960.29 crore. That’s a loss of almost Rs 33 lakh crore in investor wealth over a span of two weeks and nine trading sessions!

title: 3 workers die of suffocation while cleaning tank at Rajasthan textile factory
content: Three workers tragically died from suffocation after inhaling toxic gas while cleaning a sludge tank at Lalji Wala Mills in Balotra. The workers, identified as Gumanaram, Vishambhar Tiwari, and Shravan, were reportedly sent into the tank without proper safety equipment. An investigation is underway, with preliminary findings suggesting negligence by the factory owner.

title: What really happened to OVW referee Dallas Edwards during chaotic wrestling match?
content: Dallas Edwards, an OVW wrestling referee, suffered a medical emergency after taking a heavy hit during a match in Kentucky. The accident happened when Tony Evans pushed him into Brendan Balling’s top rope move. Edwards hit his head and later showed serious symptoms in the ring. His girlfriend later confirmed on Facebook that doctors are monitoring a brain injury.

title: 11 Indian nationals accused of staging robberies to claim crime victim visas in US
content: Eleven Indian nationals face US charges for allegedly orchestrating staged armed robberies at convenience stores. The scheme aimed to allow store clerks to falsely claim victim status on immigration applications for U Visas. Several defendants were arrested across multiple states, with some appearing in federal court.

title: From swift strike to fuel fiasco: Two weeks of Iran war explained in key points
content: The conflict between the United States, Israel, and Iran intensifies. Missile strikes and drone attacks continue across the Middle East. Oil prices surge and global markets react to the growing instability. Regional powers are increasingly drawn into the fighting. The war has spread across multiple fronts, with casualties mounting.

title: ‘India is our friend’: Iran envoy hints at 'safe passage' for Indian ships via Strait of Hormuz
content: Iran's Ambassador to India indicated that Indian vessels could soon receive safe passage through regional maritime routes, emphasizing strong bilateral ties. This development comes amid escalating tensions in West Asia affecting shipping and energy supplies through the Strait of Hormuz. Discussions are also underway for Indian ships to transport energy resources.

title: Nikki Bella explains why rumored romance with Eagles rookie Cooper DeJean collapsed
content: Nikki Bella has addressed the rumors linking her to Philadelphia Eagles cornerback Cooper DeJean. Speaking with Lala Kent, the WWE Hall of Famer said the nearly 20-year age gap and heavy online attention made a serious relationship unrealistic. Bella said they spent time together but never dated seriously, and she still believes DeJean is “an incredible guy.”

title: Caught between Hezbollah and Israel: Prof who lost colleagues
content: Two professors were killed in a bombing at Beirut's Lebanese University, leaving faculty like Habib Al-Badawi deeply shaken. He described daily life on campus as overtaken by anxiety, with routines fraught with uncertainty due to the ongoing conflict. Despite the immense psychological toll, he noted people finding ways to support each other, hoping for peace.

title: Internet claims recent viral video of Israeli PM 'AI-generated' as it shows '6 fingers'
content: A video of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has ignited a social media storm. Users on X claim the footage is AI-generated, pointing to distorted hands as evidence. The viral tweets and videos highlight growing public suspicion of digital media amid geopolitical tensions and Iran vs US-Israel war. The incident underscores the challenges of discerning truth in an era of synthetic content and information warfare.

title: Dube, who had to travel by train after T20 WC title, makes emotional post
content: Shivam Dube shared an emotional Instagram post after India’s ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026 win, placing his medal around his father’s neck and calling him "The real hero of my life." After contributing to the title triumph, including a quick 26 in the final against the New Zealand national cricket team, Dube returned home by train as flights were unavailable.

title: What are the changes being proposed to Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Act, 2019 | Explained
content: How is the government proposing to alter the definition of “transgender person”? Why has it argued for the omission of the right to “self-perceived gender identity”? What about the proposed changes have raised concerns among the trans community? What has the Supreme Court laid down in the landmark 2014 judgement recognising a third gender beyond male and female and the right to self-determination of gender identity?

title: India roots for benefits system at pandemic agreement talks
content: India has found common cause with a coalition of developing countries that says developing countries that share pathogen materials and genetic sequence data must receive fair, concrete, and legally enforceable benefits in return

title: CM Mamata announces development boards for marginalised communities ahead of Bengal Assembly polls
content: The announcement comes days after President Droupadi Murmu stated that the West Bengal government was not serious about development of Scheduled Tribes in the State

title: Restored Kamarajar Salai in Arcot town opened for public use
content: Municipal officials said that a key feature of the restoration of the stretch was an increase in its total height by two feet to the existing level of stormwater drains; this is to avoid inundation

title: New District Munsif and Judicial Magistrate Court near Katpadi inaugurated
content: Officials of the Public Works Department, which completed the work, said that the new court will function inside the refurbished panchayat office premises in the village

title: Udhayanidhi Stalin to inaugurate lounge for construction workers, village knowledge centre in Tirupattur
content: Spread over 350 sq.ft, the all-weather lounge is fitted with fans, benches, mobile phone charging units, and drinking water facilities

title: Congregational prayers, rallies held in Kashmir on Ramzan’s last Friday; Jamia Masjid closed
content: The Mirwaiz said it’s for the seventh consecutive year that the rulers have denied Muslims the right to offer prayers at Jama Masjid on this auspicious day

title: Seven dead, 92 inmates infected with herpes virus at overcrowded Jalpaiguri correctional home
content: About 92 inmates have been infected in the outbreak of the Herpes Simplex Virus between August 20, 2025 and March 9, 2026. Seven persons infected by the virus have passed away.  The number of inmates at correctional home with a capacity 700 is over 1200

title: West Asia crisis: The Indians caught in the crossfire
content: Since February 28, when Israel and the U.S. jointly attacked Iran, plunging the entire West Asian region into war, thousands of Indian tourists and expatriates have been left stranded across the Gulf. Indian missions have struggled to keep up with requests and demands. From missile and drone strikes, to soaring prices, to communication blackouts, the conflict has left civilians vulnerable, reports Kallol Bhattacherjee

title: Retention rate below 50% in BMC schools, finds report
content: According to the report, titled ‘State of Municipal Education in Mumbai’, during the period, the transition of students from Class 8 to Class 9 declined by 30%, and only 80% of students moved from Class 9 to Class 10

title: Exercise LAMITIYE-2026: India and Seychelles carry out joint military exercise
content: A series of professional interactions, tactical discussions and practical drills have been conducted as part of the exercise. Operational demonstrations have been a key component of LAMITIYE-2026.

title: IndiGo to levy fuel charge for domestic and international flights from March 14
content: IndiGo said it would continue to monitor the situation and make relevant adjustments as and when appropriate.

title: Opposition leaders press government, Lok Sabha Speaker to revoke the suspension of 8 MPs
content: The eight MPs, including seven from the Congress, have been barred from attending the remainder of the Budget Session

title: Futuristic mindfulness city fuels Bhutan’s ‘fly east’ via Assam
content: Drukair says the commencement of Paro-Singapore flight via Guwahati makes commercial sense because of altitude-dictated weight restrictions that force aeroplanes to take off from or land at Paro Airport with less fuel

title: Kerala Cabinet approves Film Policy
content: It was prepared after discussions with stakeholders in the film industry, ideas shared at the Film Conclave and opinions gathered from the public

title: VCK hopes to keep its ‘recognised party’ tag
content: Party leaders also feel a higher allotment of seats would justify its consistent support to the DMK front since 2017

title: Nitish Kumar hints at Samrat Choudhary as his successor in Bihar
content: During the ongoing Samriddhi Yatra in Saharsa, the Chief Minister placed his hands on Mr. Choudhary’s shoulders and urged the public to show their support to the Deputy CM

title: Fuel crisis: hoteliers advised to trim menus, cut operating hours
content: Finance Minister suggests alternate day operations as hotel bodies seek at least 50% quota of commercial LPG cylinders to sustain operations in the State

title: Kerala Cabinet approves formulation of land assignment rules for use of pattaya land for other purposes
content: Fees will be waived for houses and for buildings constructed for agricultural and educational purposes, as well as markets, places of worship, structures for public purposes, cultural institutions, clubs and structures for charitable establishments

title: Haryana Rajya Sabha polls: Congress MLAs reach Himachal Pradesh to prevent cross-voting
content: After CLP meet, about 30 MLAs leave for Shimla; party insiders say the move is meant to keep the flock together ahead of three-cornered election; Congress holds 37 seats and needs 31 votes to send its candidate

title: 27 Indian-flagged ships remain stranded in Persian Gulf, one starts moving: Government official
content: Jag Prakash, an oil tanker with a carrying capacity of 48,000 tonnes, is headed to Tanzania, the official says; three cargo ships with 76 seafarers aboard are located in the Gulf of Oman, while 24 ships are stranded in the Persian Gulf; there are 23,000 Indian nationals working in the region on merchant ships, ports and offshore vessels

title: How many constituencies can a candidate contest from? | TN Elections with The Hindu
content:
In the 2001 Tamil Nadu Assembly elections, AIADMK supremo Jayalalithaa filed nominations from four seats and 
all four nominations were rejected. Protests erupted. The ruling government was accused of conspiracy.
And ten years later, the Supreme Court pulled up that leader sharply. That leader was AIADMK supremo Jayalalithaa. How many constituencies can a candidate legally contest from in India?

title: In Rajya Sabha, BJP MP proposes setting up National Commission for persons with disabilities
content: MP Bhim Singh said that the architecture of such a National Commission should be similar to other National Commissions, have “independent powers” and have regional offices to ensure accessibility.

title: Collector reviews LPG stocks at Kondapalli bottling plant
content: Lakshmisha assures public there is no shortage as oil companies can supply over 25,000 cylinders daily against demand of 16,000 in Vijayawada; warns of action against black-marketing and rumour-mongering

title: PM distributes land deeds to Assam’s tea workers
content: In the poll-bound State, PM Narendra Modi says plantation workers had to wait years for land rights because Congress never cared for them during their tenure; this is the first such initiative in 200 years of Assam tea

title: Supreme Court declines plea to make nucleic acid tests compulsory at blood banks
content: NAT tests are more expensive but more accurate in detecting HIV, Hepatitis B, C viruses in the blood; petitioner sought to protect thalassemia patients who require frequent blood transfusions and are at risk from infected blood

title: IPL 2026 in Bengaluru likely to get nod after stadium safety overhaul
content: Home Minister G. Parameshwara is likely to announce the clearance on Monday (March 16) after a meeting with the committee where the final report will be submitted.

title: Kerala HC directs to defer nurses’ strike till March 19, suggests mediation
content: Stating that any continuing difference of opinion between the nurses and private hospital managements will affect the people seeking treatment and other members of the public, the court says that it will be ideal if both sides choose mediation

title: Bengaluru shows consistently low PM2.5 levels compared to other cities, says report
content: Seasonal mean PM2.5 concentrations in Bengaluru indicate comparatively better air quality than northern cities, with clear seasonal variability across 2024 and 2025

title: Tracing Hyderabad’s centuries-old Persian connections
content: From the dargah of Ibne Khatoon to Irani cafes and Muharram majlis, Hyderabad’s mourning for Iran’s leader reflects centuries-old cultural connections forged by Persian migrants, Qutb Shahi rulers, diplomats, and traders who transformed the Deccan’s language, food, and traditions

title: Focus is on modernising agriculture, improving farmers’ income, says Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu
content: An amount of ₹2,670 crore has been credited to the accounts of 46.85 lakh farmers towards third instalment of the PM Kisan-Annadata Sukhibhava programme, and this includes the State’s share of ₹1,874 crore

title: NHRC issues notices to five States over rise in missing persons cases
content: The Commission takes cognisance of media reports indicating 12,000 and 14,000 missing persons cases annually since 2013 in Bihar alone; observes the issue raises serious concerns of human rights violations

title: Cong. MLA B.R. Patil, another accused of committing irregularities in development works
content: Subhash Guttedar says that crores of rupees have been misappropriated through poor execution of road works in Aland constituency

title: Thiruvananthapuram Collector’s report contradicts Corporation’s claim on Attukal Pongala cleanup
content: While the Mayor had said that cleaning was completed within a few hours of the festival, the Collector’s report points out that the waste removal was completed only within three days

title: Kerala government decides to lift construction ban in Idukki
content: Title deed holders can now build homes and utilise their land. Individuals can construct up to 5,000 sq ft without any fees, while commercial projects will incur charges based on land fair value. Activist express concerns that the timing may be politically motivated ahead of upcoming Assembly elections.

title: Congress stages protest outside ITC factory near Yelwal over tobacco price crash
content: Karnataka Pradesh Congress Committee spokesperson M. Lakshman says tobacco prices, which had dropped to around ₹240 per kg after companies scaled down procurement, showed signs of recovery on March 13.

title: Kerala Assembly Polls 2026 | Sudhakaran hints at ‘revolt’ as Congress decides to keep MPs off Assembly polls
content: Sudhakaran’s ‘hasty return’ from Delhi to Kannur, when Parliament is in session, seems to have rocked the Congress’s bid to present a united face at the hustings. He asserted last week that he would ‘100 per cent” contest the polls from Kannur

title: School examination wings come under a single umbrella at Pareeksha Bhavan
content: Minister inaugurates new block for higher secondary and vocational higher secondary examination wings at Pareeksha Bhavan

title: Firewood to the rescue as LPG goes scarce
content: Many restaurants in the outskirts of Thiruvananthapuram remain untouched by the shortage of LPG cylinders since they have for long been operating with firewood stoves.

title: Support Yathindra as you supported me, Siddaramaiah tells Varuna voters
content: He says he might not be able to visit Varuna constituency frequently; adds that his son will take care of the constituency in his absence.

title: Bradley beats buzzer to put Arizona in Big 12 final
content: Jaden Bradley hit a fall-away jumper at the buzzer to send No. 2 Arizona to the Big 12 tournament finals with an 82-80 win over Iowa State on Friday night.

title: Leafs star Matthews (knee) to miss rest of season
content: Maple Leafs captain Auston Matthews has a torn MCL in his left knee and will miss the rest of the NHL season.

title: WNBPA hopeful as sides 'chip away' in CBA talks
content: WNBPA chief Terri Carmichael Jackson expressed optimism on Day 4 of CBA negotiations with the league, saying that continued conversations have helped "chip away at what the concerns are for both sides."

title: MAC commish predicts 2 bids after Miami (OH) loss
content: MAC commissioner Jon A. Steinbrecher is confident his conference will get two teams into the NCAA tournament for the first time in 27 years despite Miami (Ohio) losing to UMass in the league quarterfinals after going 31-0 in the regular season.

title: Seahawks GM: State tax law could impact club
content: Seattle Seahawks general manager John Schneider said he believed Washington state's new income tax on high earners would eventually have a negative impact on the team's ability to sign free agents.

title: McIlroy says back improving, 'happy' to make cut
content: Rory McIlroy wasn't sure he could play in the Players Championship because of back spasms. Now he's happy to get to play for two more days.

title: Falcons' GM: Tua to compete with Penix for QB job
content: Falcons general manager Ian Cunningham said definitively Friday that the team is bringing in Tua Tagovailoa to compete with Michael Penix Jr. for the starting quarterback job.

title: Men's Bracketology: The SEC teams that will sweat out Selection Sunday
content: Texas, Oklahoma and Auburn are all hanging around the cutline. Only the Sooners can currently control their destiny.

title: Men's Bubble Watch: Ohio State looks safe despite loss, Seton Hall does not
content: The Buckeyes' NCAA tournament outlook remains positive despite falling short in a close Big Ten quarterfinal.

title: Women's Bracketology: Champ Week upsets ripple through the field
content: Dreams are being realized and crushed during Champ Week. Stephen F. Austin pulled the latest upset to clinch an auto-bid.

title: Women's freshman impact rankings: The top 10 (plus 1) to end the regular season
content: Five players crack the final rankings, but none unseat Jazzy Davidson or Aliyah Chavez from Nos. 1 and 2.

title: Where nine of the best remaining NFL free agents should sign: We found perfect landing spots
content: Where should Jauan Jennings, Joey Bosa and Rasheed Walker sign? Which roster needs Kirk Cousins? Let's find new homes for nine players still looking for one.

title: Live tracking NFL free agency: Updates on signings, trades, cuts
content: We're following NFL free agency signings, trades, cuts, contract restructures and other moves as they happen.

title: NHL playoff watch: Projections for the Oilers' postseason path
content: Here's how it's shaping up for Edmonton. Plus, full Stanley Cup playoff projections, draft lottery standings and more.

title: Weekend predictions: Can Spurs stop record losing streak at Liverpool?
content: It's Friday, and another full weekend of action awaits across the soccer calendar as Julien Laurens picks out the best games to watch in Europe.

title: NBA draft intel and latest buzz: Peterson or Dybantsa at No. 1? What's Boozer's case?
content: Here's what league insiders are saying about Darryn Peterson, AJ Dybantsa and more top NBA draft prospects.

title: Can the Knicks get Towns, Brunson on track? Five issues they need to solve -- fast
content: The Knicks are 42-25 and third in the Eastern Conference, but they have a few problems to solve if they want to make a deep playoff run.

title: Men's coaching tracker: Who could be up next at Cincinnati?
content: The Bearcats are moving on from Wes Miller after five seasons -- but have promising candidates on deck.

title: What Kyler Murray signing means for Vikings, J.J. McCarthy -- and Justin Jefferson's fantasy value
content: Will Murray resurrect his career and boost the Vikings' offense after being released by the Cardinals?

title: Full 2026 draft order: When teams will pick across all 257 selections, seven rounds
content: The order for all seven rounds of the 2026 NFL draft is set, starting with the Raiders at No. 1 and ending with the Broncos at No. 257.

title: 'Just wild': Bam Adebayo's 83-point performance resonated with his AAU coach
content: Ty White said his former high school star did something to wow him every game, but even this performance has him stunned.

title: NWSL 2026 mega-preview: Key moves, questions and predictions for all 16 teams
content: The NWSL is back! Let's predict how each team will finish by assessing what's ahead for Rodman, Lavelle and the other stars.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Chelsea winger Pedro Neto on Barcelona's radar
content: Barcelona are monitoring Chelsea's Pedro Neto in case they decide to strengthen at winger. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Hamilton: Ferrari losing 'a lot of time' to Mercedes
content: Lewis Hamilton has said Ferrari's gap to Mercedes is mainly due to a deficit in power unit performance after he qualified fourth for Saturday's Chinese Grand Prix sprint race.

title: Haney vs. Garcia 2? Mayweather vs. Tyson still happening?
content: Andreas Hale answers some key questions to bring clarity to the most intriguing storylines in boxing today.

title: Risers and fallers: Filipowski, da Silva moving up; Flagg a problem during playoffs?
content: Tristan da Silva is on the rise, and should fantasy basketball managers keep players like Bennedict Mathurin and Cameron Johnson?

title: Cheat sheet: Sleepers, busts, rookies and closers
content: You've read their names all preseason long, it'd be a shame to forget them on draft day. The ESPN Cheat Sheet is one way to make sure that doesn't happen.

title: Guide to NFL offseason signings: Can Geno Smith rebound from 2025 in New York?
content: Your one-stop shop for fantasy football reaction to all the key player movement via free agency and trades.

title: Government bypassing all norms to appoint body builders as police officers: K.C. Venugopal
content: e-Paper The View From India Looking at World Affairs from the Indian perspective . first day first show news and reviews from the world of cinema and streaming . the weekly newsletter from science writers takes the jargon out of science and puts the fun in!

title: Leaders of AIADMK, allies to participate in State-wide demos on March 17 against DMK govt.
content: e-Paper The View From India Looking at World Affairs from the Indian perspective . first day first show news and reviews from the world of cinema and streaming . the weekly newsletter from science writers takes the jargon out of science and puts the fun in!

title: Trump Says US Bombed Iran's Kharg Island, Threatens Oil Infrastructure
content: US President Donald Trump on Friday Said on Friday that the United States carried out strikes against "every military target" on Iran's Kharg Island export hub.

title: US-Israel Iran War LIVE Updates: All Crew Members Of US KC-135 Loss In Iraq Confirmed Dead
content: US-Israel Iran Attacks LIVE Updates: Buildings in Dubai shook after loud explosions were heard, and a thick cloud of smoke rose over a central part of the city.

title: 5 US Air Force Planes Damaged In Iranian Strike On Saudi Arabia: Reports
content: Five US Air Force refueling planes were struck and damaged on the ground at Prince Sultan air base in Saudi Arabia, the Wall Street Journal reported on Friday, citing two US officials.

title: Ready For Long Battle As Israel Threatens Lebanese Infrastructure: Hezbollah
content: Lebanon was drawn into the Middle East war last week when the Tehran-backed militant group attacked Israel in response to the killing of Iranian supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in US-Israeli strikes.

title: US Deploys B-2 Bombers For Operation Against Iran
content: The United States Central Command (CENTCOM) on Friday said B-2 stealth bombers have taken off to carry out a mission as part of Operation Epic Fury, aimed at "eliminating threats" posed by the Iranian "regime".

title: US Sending 2,500 Marines To Middle East As Iran War Intensifies
content: About 2,500 US Marines are being deployed to the Middle East as American and Israeli strikes keep pounding Iran and the Islamic Republic keeps attacking Persian Gulf shipping and energy infrastructure.

title: Delhi Coaching Deaths: Court Asks CBI To Probe MCD Corrupt Practices
content: The Rouse Avenue Court on Friday directed the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) to conduct further investigation into the issues of dereliction of duties and corrupt practices by the MCD officials.

title: Former Prince Andrew, Ex-UK Envoy Pictured In Bathrobes With Epstein
content: A photograph of Britain's former prince Andrew and veteran politician Peter Mandelson sitting in bathrobes alongside late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein was unearthed Friday in previously published documents.

title: The Process To Impeach Poll Body Chief And How Opposition Is Prepared
content: Reasons cited in notice for impeachment of CEC include large-scale deletion of voters' names in Bihar and Bengal during SIR, biased attitude towards certain political parties, alleged misconduct on part of Gyanesh Kumar

title: Exclusive: Senior Maoist Leader Denies Surrender, Says He Was Arrested
content: Thippiri Tirupathi -- a former Maoist leader and the next in line for the general secretary post in the banned Communist Party of India (Maoist) -- has claimed that the Telangana government's claim of their recent "surrender" -- is misleading.

title: "Missiles, Drones Flying Over Us": LPG-Laden Indian Tanker Stuck Near Hormuz
content: The tanker -- under the command of Captain Virendra Vishwakarma -- is loaded with enough LPG to fill 3.6 million domestic gas cylinders.

title: Gangster Groom Arrested From Mandap, Bride Begs Cops To Let Wedding Be Over
content: What was expected to be a day of celebration turned into a crime drama in Bhopal when police stormed a wedding ceremony and arrested the groom, a gangster, from the mandap.

title: Global Supply Chain Disruptions Highlight Need For Resilient Infrastructure: Karan Adani
content: As global supply chains face recurring disruptions from geopolitical tensions and shifting trade routes, resilient infrastructure and logistics networks will be critical to sustaining economic growth, said Karan Adani.

title: Dubai Introduces Tougher Oversight Law Amid Rising Middle East Tensions
content: Against a backdrop of rising regional tensions and recent security alerts, the leadership in Dubai has introduced a significant new legal framework.

title: Rs 92,47,48,000: The Bounty US Has Set For Information On Mojtaba Khamenei
content: The United States has offered a reward of up to $10 million or Rs 92,47,48,000 at current exchange rates for information on Iran's new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, and several other senior officials tied to the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps.

title: While Bhopal Waits In Line, The LPG Black Market Cashes In
content: The illegal market is flourishing at a time when ordinary consumers say getting a cylinder through official channels has become increasingly difficult.

title: 2 Explosive Drones Intercepted In Islamabad Days After Afghan Threat: Report
content: Pakistani security authorities on Friday intercepted and destroyed two explosive-laden drones in the capital Islamabad, according to a media report.

title: From Japan To Spain: Countries That Provide Menstrual Leaves
content: Chief Justice Surya Kant said on Friday that mandatory menstrual leave for women could "adversely impact" their employment prospects.

title: As Srinagar's Jamia Masjid Closed During Ramzan, Mirwaiz Farooq's "Our Hearts Cry" Remark
content: The iconic Jamia Masjid Srinagar was closed by authorities on Friday as thousands were expected to attend Jum-atul- Wida ( Farewell Friday congregation) of the fasting month of Ramzan.

title: Home Ministry To Review VIP Security Measures Days After Farooq Abdullah Attack
content: Farooq Abdullah had a miraculous escape on Wednesday night when a gunman shot at him from behind as the National Conference president was leaving a wedding in Jammu.

title: Sleep disorders putting lakhs of Indians at risk: Doctors
content: Anuja Jaiswal is a Senior Assistant Editor at The Times of India, with an impressive 18-year career in narrative journalism. She specializes in health and heritage reporting, expertly simplifying complex health information to make it engaging and understandable for readers. Her deep dives into heritage topics are well-researched, resulting in captivating narratives that resonate with her audience. Over the years, she has worked in Chandigarh, Chhattisgarh and West UP, gaining diverse on-ground experience that shapes her storytelling. Read More
